marketState: neutral — bearish technicals & storage surplus offset by extreme-short COT positioning and surging 8-16D weather demand
summary: Price ($2.87) sits below its typical seasonal path (-6.2pp vs median) and inside a wide, low-reliability fair-value band, currently rated FAIR against a $2.92 fair value that rises toward $3.03 by +21 days. All six technical timeframes are aligned bearish (EMA50<200, negative MACD) and storage printed a bearish +17 Bcf surprise with a +183 Bcf surplus vs the 5y average, arguing for near-term softness. Working against that: managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range (a crowded, squeeze-prone position) and 8-16 day weather demand is running +51.7% vs 5y and above the historical range max, pointing to a strong cooling-demand pulse ahead. Seasonality odds tilt mildly positive (69% higher in 1wk and 4wk) but reliability is explicitly flagged Low. Net: near-term momentum favors the downside, but medium-term positioning and weather skew argue for mean reversion higher.

longTerm.sizing: 1R ≈ 1% account risk; trim size given Low seasonality reliability

Fair value itself is rising ($2.92 now to $3.03 by +21D) while front price sits at a growing discount to it, and seasonality odds lean higher over 1 and 4 weeks.
longTerm.invalidation: COT net-short percentile resets higher without any price follow-through, 8-16D weather demand outlook reverts to normal/below-normal, or storage surprises stay bearish and the surplus vs 5y widens further.
